---
title: "Please help! - computer not booting after installing Ubuntu and running boot-repair"
date: 2013-07-10
forum: Installation &amp; Upgrades
---

### Post by tianyuan on 2013-07-10
Hello,
Here's my problem: I originally had Linux Mint 14 installed on my Lenovo Ideapad Y500, on which I tried to resize the disk partitions using a Parted Magic live usb. It seemed to have messed up my grub settings, so I decided to install Ubuntu 12.04.2 LTS on the laptop, hoping it could solve all problems. The installation was successful, and I ran boot-repair after installation. After that I tried to reboot my laptop, but I just got a black screen and couldn't boot into anything. 

I have a 16G SSD and a 1TB HHD, and partitioned the SSD into three parts, /boot,/, and /usr. I had the bootloader installed on this SSD in the installation. Here's part of the BootInfo Script I got from boot-repair:
 
[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/image/png;base64,[/IMG]

[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/image/png;base64,[/IMG]

[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/image/png;base64,[/IMG]
 

The whole script can be seen at [paste.ubuntu.com/5863198]("https://paste.ubuntu.com/5863198"). Sorry I'm not pasting it here. It's a bit too long.

 Thank you for your help!

---

### Post by grahammechanical on 2013-07-10
First of all the pastebin image cannot be seen. It is because you have set the address to https://   when it needs to be http://

Second, what disk has the boot priority? Is it sda or sdb? It needs to be sda that has the boot priority. 

Thirdly, I am curious as to why you ran boot repair when you said the install of 12,04,2 was successful?

Regards.

---

### Post by oldfred on 2013-07-11
Welcome to the forums.

(I took s out and could see pastebin, but better if you fix link)
You have a BIOS install, with lots of separate system partitions, some on SSD and others on HD. HD is gpt partitioned, but SSD is MBR(msdos) partitioned. Might be better if never installing Windows to convert SSD to gpt also, but you would have to reinstall, so not critical.

I think all these were UEFI installs.
 Lenovo Ideapad Y500 LiveUSB Problem
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2095063](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2095063)
[http://askubuntu.com/questions/272570/unable-to-install-ubuntu-on-lenovo-y500](http://askubuntu.com/questions/272570/unable-to-install-ubuntu-on-lenovo-y500)
[http://askubuntu.com/questions/272570/unable-to-install-ubuntu-on-lenovo-y500/290358#290358](http://askubuntu.com/questions/272570/unable-to-install-ubuntu-on-lenovo-y500/290358#290358)

But I think your issues may be related to the dual video. If your BIOS lets you set one or the other then it is easier.

---
